heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 07 September 2025 when 23 were seen.

storm.status: A storm is passing through the area.  This marks the first storm since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 23 December 2025, is ranked 7th among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 9 days so far.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (197°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 8.2°C), outside temperature (down 5.0°C), and feels like temperature (down 4.2°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 287 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.8°C), outside temperature (up 9.0°C), cloud levels (up 325 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 286.9m lower than yesterday, 325.1m lower than the past week, 31.6m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 828m to a maximum of 2280m, averaging 994.0m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1319.1m and 962.4m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: The sky may alternate between sunny and overcast.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; extremely cold temperatures will prevail. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and very little to no wind will be present. Drizzle or light rain possible, the sky may alternate between sunny and overcast, and moderate levels of sunlight will be present. High pressure will dominate, leading to stable weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-5.7°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
